title: Fluentd / Fluent Bit Buffer Overflow & Retry Exhaustion (file/memory-buffer queue saturation, retry-wait backoff exhaustion, out-of-buffer events, plugin-emit failures, flush-thread starvation)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.7.4 Fluentd worker crash and supervisor restart detection, UC-13.2.10 ITSI-centric Fluentd plugin error rollups without raw buffer lanes, deep Splunk indexer queue internals as a primary signal, or cloud load balancer analytics absent shipper telemetry. Does not automatically mutate Fluentd or Fluent Bit configuration; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved pipeline changes.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation with fifteen minute schedule; platform logging dashboard drilldowns on shipper sourcetypes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab logging namespace, pause a non-production Splunk HEC token or block outbound TLS to Elasticsearch while Fluentd monitor_agent polls continue, confirm rising buffer_queue_length and retry_count in sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent with matching emit transaction failed lines in sourcetype=fluentd:log, and verify observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation returns page or warn with non-null recommended_action within thirty minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when curl /api/plugins.json shows stable queues, retries fall near zero, fluentbit:metrics reports low retries_failed, stderr is quiet, and kube events show no BackOff for fluent pods, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

dataSources: (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent (in_monitor_agent /api/plugins.json polls; buffer_queue_length buffer_total_queued_size buffer_queue_byte_size retry_count emit_records emit_count); sourcetype=fluentd:log (BufferOverflowError emit transaction failed failed to flush the buffer); sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics (/api/v1/metrics JSON; retries retries_failed chunks_overlimit signals); sourcetype=fluentbit:log ([error] [output:*] no upstream connections available); sourcetype=kube:events (fluentd fluent-bit pods BackOff Unhealthy); lookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv keyed on node or host for cluster_tier owner_team environment
app: Splunk Connect for Kubernetes / Splunk Enterprise (HEC) + custom in_http output
spl:
`comment("UC-13.7.1 Fluentd monitor_agent + Fluent Bit metrics multisearch lanes HEC-ES-Kafka correlation; earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,hostname,fluentd_host,""))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,plugin_type,out_plugin,"fluentd_out")))
      | eval buf_q=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_length,queue_length,q_size,buffer_queue_byte_size,"0")),10)
      | eval buf_cap=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_limit,queue_limit,max_queued_chunks,buffer_length_limit,"0")),10)
      | eval fill_pct=if(buf_cap>0,round(100*buf_q/nullif(buf_cap,0),2),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(percent_full,pct_queued,"0")),10))
      | eval retry_cnt=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,retries,plugin_retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_pct=if(retry_cnt<=0,0,round(min(100,retry_cnt/3),2))
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(record_age_sec,oldest_chunk_age_sec,"0")),10)/60
      | eval lane="fluentd_monitor"
      | eval raw_sig="fdm:".plugin_id.":q=".tostring(round(coalesce(buf_q,0),0))
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,hostname,""))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"bufferoverflow|buffer.?overflow|bufferoverflowerror|emit transaction failed|failed to flush the buffer|out of buffer")
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"fluentd_log_line")
      | eval fill_pct=92
      | eval retry_cnt=if(match(lr,"retry|exhaust|giving up|too many error"),400,25)
      | eval retry_pct=if(retry_cnt>=100,88,22)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentd_log"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(_raw,1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,name,pod_name,hostname,""))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_name,output,input_name,"fluentbit_metrics")))
      | eval r_ok=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries,output_retries,retry_total,"0")),10)
      | eval r_bad=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries_failed,retries_failed_count,errors,output_dropped_records,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_pct=if(r_ok+r_bad>0,round(100*r_bad/nullif(r_ok+r_bad,0),2),0)
      | eval fill_pct=if(match(lower(_raw),"chunks_overlimit|chunk.*overlimit|overlimit"),97,if(retry_pct>=35,82,18))
      | eval retry_cnt=coalesce(r_bad,r_ok)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_metrics"
      | eval raw_sig="fbm:".plugin_id.":fail=".tostring(coalesce(r_bad,0))
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentbit:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,pod_name,""))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"no upstream|upstream connections|failed to flush|output.*error|chunk")
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_name,section,"fluentbit_output_log")
      | eval fill_pct=88
      | eval retry_cnt=if(match(lr,"no upstream"),777,55)
      | eval retry_pct=if(match(lr,"no upstream"),99,41)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_log"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(_raw,1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=kube:events earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval inv=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name,involved_object_name,name,""))))
      | where match(inv,"fluentd|fluent-bit|fluentbit")
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason,Reason,Type,type,"")))
      | where match(lower(reason),"backoff|unhealthy|failed|oom|evicted")
      | eval shipper_node=inv
      | eval plugin_id="kube_evt:".reason
      | eval fill_pct=72
      | eval retry_cnt=160
      | eval retry_pct=55
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="kube_events"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(message,Message,""))),1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
| eval shipper_node=if(len(shipper_node)==0,"unknown_shipper",shipper_node)
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".shipper_node."|".plugin_id."|".lane
| sort 0 + cluster + shipper_node + plugin_id + lane - _time
| streamstats window=9 current=t global=f count AS burn_window BY merge_key
| eval fill_pct=coalesce(fill_pct,0)
| eval retry_cnt=coalesce(retry_cnt,0)
| eval retry_pct=coalesce(retry_pct,0)
| eventstats perc95(fill_pct) AS cluster_fill_p95 BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(fill_pct) AS fill_pct max(retry_pct) AS retry_pct max(retry_cnt) AS retry_cnt max(oldest_record_age_min) AS oldest_record_age_min max(burn_window) AS burn_window max(cluster_fill_p95) AS cluster_fill_p95 first(raw_sig) AS cond_msg first(lane) AS coalesce_lane BY cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane
| eventstats sum(eval(if(fill_pct>=88 OR retry_pct>=70 OR retry_cnt>=500,1,0))) AS cluster_hotspot_cnt BY cluster
| eval severity=case(fill_pct>=98 OR retry_cnt>=800 OR retry_pct>=95,"page", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_log|fluentbit_log") AND (match(cond_msg,"BufferOverflow|no upstream|giving up|failed to flush") OR retry_cnt>=300),"page", fill_pct>=85 OR retry_cnt>=120 OR retry_pct>=50,"warn", cluster_hotspot_cnt>=5,"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_monitor"),"curl_localhost_24220_api_plugins_compare_buffer_fields_then_validate_downstream_splunk_hec", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_log"),"trace_emit_flush_errors_disk_io_and_output_tls_hec_es_kafka_s3", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentbit_metrics"),"inspect_fluentbit_storage_chunks_scheduler_and_http_pool_limits", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentbit_log"),"validate_upstream_elasticsearch_kafka_network_policy_and_dns", match(coalesce_lane,"kube_events"),"kubectl_describe_pod_events_check_oom_backoff_probes", true(),"refresh_all_log_shipper_telemetry_lanes")
| join type=left max=0 shipper_node
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,""))))
     | fields shipper_node cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| table cluster shipper_node plugin_id coalesce_lane fill_pct retry_pct oldest_record_age_min burn_window cluster_hotspot_cnt cluster_fill_p95 severity recommended_action cond_msg cluster_tier owner_team environment
description: Detects Fluentd buffer plugin saturation and retry exhaustion using in_monitor_agent style queue and retry counters plus diagnostic log lines, alongside Fluent Bit /api/v1/metrics backlog and retries_failed signals, stderr output starvation messages, and Kubernetes events for fluent pods, then correlates severity with Splunk HEC, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and S3 downstream health context enriched by node inventory.
value: Platform teams shorten mean time to innocence when applications look fine yet logs arrive late or vanish: rising queues and exhausted retries usually precede user-visible gaps, and distinguishing memory versus file buffers prevents mis-tuning that trades away durability. Risk and engineering governance groups receive a tamper-resistant ledger that ties structured monitor polls, engine logs, metrics scrapes, and cluster events into one searchable narrative across destinations.
implementation: Land sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent JSON from /api/plugins.json polls, sourcetype=fluentd:log engine lines, sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics from /api/v1/metrics scrapes, sourcetype=fluentbit:log stderr, sourcetype=kube:events for fluent pods, and join fluentd_node_inventory.csv; save observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation every fifteen minutes over earliest=-4h@h; route page and warn via platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports to a restricted index.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster and shipper_node with drilldowns to fluentd:monitor_agent JSON, fluentd:log lines, fluentbit:metrics documents, fluentbit:log stderr, kube:events messages, and inventory context; single-value tiles for cluster_hotspot_cnt; timeline of fill_pct and retry_pct.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_host
| join type=left max=0 shipper_host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval shipper_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,""))))
     | fields shipper_host cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| where cpu_load>0 OR mem_used>0
| table shipper_host cpu_load mem_used cluster_tier owner_team environment
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for hosts that run Fluentd or Fluent Bit when you map queue and CPU signals into Performance.host; retain thirty days for quarterly platform attestations and capacity reviews.

references.title: Fluent Bit Documentation — Getting Started with Fluent Bit
Fluent Bit Documentation — Buffering and Storage
CNCF Blog — Fluent Bit graduates with a focus on scalable observability pipelines

knownFalsePositives: Approved downstream maintenance on Splunk indexers, Elasticsearch data nodes, Kafka brokers, or S3 gateways routinely elevates retry_count while engineers expect bounded loss; require change_ticket_id correlation and dwell thresholds before customer-facing pages. Fluent Bit container restarts during rolling Kubernetes upgrades create transient queue spikes and benign chunks_overlimit_warning text that clears after pods become ready; suppress when rollout annotations and cluster version skew match an approved window. Scheduled S3 lifecycle pauses or legal holds can slow flush acknowledgements without indicating shipper misconfiguration; pair with object storage ticket metadata before rewriting buffer limits. Indexed-volume-scaling load events that intentionally burst hot tiers may mimic saturation until autoscale completes; compare to capacity dashboards rather than shipper logs alone. Customer-driven log replays into hot tiers can refill memory buffers quickly even when steady-state traffic is calm; confirm replay job IDs in sidecar metadata. Retry-wait exponential backoff produces slow ramps that look like false negatives in five-minute windows; widen alert evaluation or use consecutive breach counts. Expected chunks_overlimit_warning after deliberate upstream throttle drills or red-line testing should not page application teams when drill labels are present. Network MTU changes scheduled with network operations can briefly break TLS sessions to Elasticsearch or Kafka, causing retry spikes that self-heal; treat as benign when maintenance calendars and path MTU probes align. Mis-parsed timestamps from a single noisy source can inflate fill_pct math when exporter bugs double-count; validate FIELDALIAS quarterly. Duplicate HEC posts from redundant forwarders can inflate emit counters until dedupe logic lands in summary indexes.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on log-shipper entities by cluster_tier and owner_team

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team that operates Fluentd collectors on virtual machines, Fluent Bit DaemonSets on Kubernetes, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes agents, and any bespoke Ruby or Golang sidecars that terminate enterprise logging traffic before it reaches Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoints, Elasticsearch bulk APIs, Kafka log topics, or object storage sinks. This use case isolates saturation and retry exhaustion that occur while chunks still appear healthy at the process level: buffer plugins absorb bursts, output plugins serialize network writes, and the combination of volatile memory buffers plus durable file buffers creates distinct failure signatures that generic host disk alerts miss until data is already delayed or dropped.

Fluentd remains the Ruby-first reference implementation with a rich plugin ecosystem. The bundled in_monitor_agent input exposes a REST surface, commonly bound to port 24220 on loopback in hardened deployments, that returns JSON describing plugin lifecycle, buffer statistics, and retry posture. Practitioners poll /api/plugins.json or rely on Splunk scripted inputs that curl the same payload on a fixed interval. High-signal fields your exporter should flatten include buffer_queue_length, buffer_total_queued_size, buffer_queue_byte_size, retry_count, emit_records, and emit_count, alongside plugin_id or type strings that disambiguate out_forward, out_http toward Splunk HEC, out_kafka2, out_s3, and composite copy trees. Queue length tells you how many chunks wait; byte-oriented counters help when length limits and byte limits diverge; retry_count climbing while emit_records stalls implies the output thread cannot commit to the remote.

Fluent Bit is the C implementation tuned for embedded and Kubernetes footprints. Its HTTP monitoring interface, often on port 2020 when enabled, serves /api/v1/metrics as JSON summarizing per-input and per-output throughput. Typical keys include bytes and records read and written, error counters, retry totals, retries_failed, dropped records, and storage backlog hints that surface as chunks_overlimit_warning style messages when the engine cannot retire chunks quickly enough. Treat Fluent Bit storage on disk as part of the same story as Fluentd buffer_path directories: both protect at-least-once delivery until the filesystem or inode budget collapses.

buffer_memory is fast and volatile: container restarts wipe pending events unless you accept lossy modes or pair with file storage. buffer_file trades latency for durability, multiplying inode and fsync pressure on cloud root volumes or persistent volumes. Hybrid deployments use memory for hot paths and spill to file under pressure; Splunk should see the mode via monitor_agent metadata or configuration snapshots so analysts do not misread a deliberate file-backed queue as sudden regression.

Scope boundaries keep sibling gold use cases complementary rather than redundant. UC-13.7.4 focuses on Fluentd worker process crashes, supervisor respawns, and signal-driven death spirals where the Ruby parent cannot keep children alive; buffer queues may empty during those incidents in misleading ways, so this document does not replace crash-first paging. UC-13.2.10 frames Fluentd plugin error counts inside ITSI service analytics and episode grouping; it optimizes executive roll-ups rather than the multisource buffer lane fusion prescribed here. When both UCs fire, treat UC-13.7.1 as the deep buffer and retry narrative and UC-13.7.4 as the process stability narrative, merging timelines only after you confirm distinct root causes.

RBAC and safety: collectors that call Fluentd monitor_agent endpoints should use dedicated service identities confined to loopback-forwarding sidecars or administrative hosts, not interactive engineer laptops. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy should allow Fluent Bit metrics scrapes only from the Prometheus or Splunk poll namespace you designate. Secrets for Splunk HEC tokens, Elasticsearch API keys, Kafka SASL passwords, and S3 signing credentials belong in vault-backed stores with rotation telemetry correlated to retry spikes. Legal review should confirm whether raw log samples in cond_msg excerpts may contain regulated payloads; redact aggressively in pager templates.

Index design: designate a primary index such as fluent or platform for sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent structured polls, sourcetype=fluentd:log for supervisor and engine lines, sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics for JSON scrapes, and sourcetype=fluentbit:log for stderr streams. Land sourcetype=kube:events in kubernetes or platform indexes with sufficient retention for pod lifecycle forensics. Maintain fluentd_node_inventory.csv with columns node or host, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment, optional region, and optional downstream_owner so joins attach business context without ad hoc lookups during incidents.

Field normalization: coerce numeric strings with tonumber, unify cluster labels across cloud metadata, map pod_name to shipper_node when host is missing, and preserve plugin_id text case-insensitively for stability. Timestamp skew between poll intervals and log lines requires widest time windows during investigations; the saved search uses earliest=-4h@h to tolerate delayed file buffer flushes.

Risk briefing: short spikes in retry_count during downstream maintenance may be benign when change_ticket_id metadata is present; pair severity ladders with maintenance calendars. Memory-buffer overflows during deliberate traffic drills can exceed static thresholds without customer impact if canary indexes absorb load; require environment != prod style gates before paging. Elasticsearch bulk rejections often present as Fluentd emit transaction failed lines while Splunk HEC remains healthy; multisearch separation prevents misrouting blame.

Differentiation recap: buffer saturation, retry exhaustion, chunk overlimit warnings, output upstream starvation, and correlated Kubernetes BackOff signals for fluent pods, not generic worker death alone, not ITSI-only plugin rollups without raw lanes.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse, plus an optional sixth scripted poll of /api/plugins.json when monitor_agent JSON is not yet mapped into sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent.

Fluentd monitor_agent lane: schedule a curl or Splunk modular input every sixty to one hundred twenty seconds against http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json on each collector host or sidecar, forwarding JSON to HTTP Event Collector with sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent. Flatten each plugin object into one event per plugin_id, carrying buffer_queue_length, buffer_total_queued_size, buffer_queue_byte_size, retry_count, emit_records, emit_count, and queue_time statistics when exposed. Include shipper_role tags such as daemonset, deployment, or vm_agent for routing.

Fluentd log lane: ship /var/log/fluent/fluentd.log or container stdout with sourcetype=fluentd:log. Ensure LINE_BREAKER preserves multi-line Ruby stack traces. Capture BufferOverflowError, emit transaction failed, failed to flush the buffer, and chunk full warnings with TRUNCATE set generously for stack dumps.

Fluent Bit metrics lane: enable HTTP metrics in the Fluent Bit configuration, scrape /api/v1/metrics with Prometheus or Splunk scripted input, and land JSON lines as sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics. Retain input and output record counters, error counters, retry and retries_failed fields, storage layer metrics, and any chunks_overlimit_warning text embedded in logs mirrored into metrics when your exporter concatenates both.

Fluent Bit log lane: capture stderr with sourcetype=fluentbit:log, focusing on [error] [output:*] no upstream connections available, TLS handshake failures, and scheduler warnings. Namespace pod labels should include app.kubernetes.io/name for drilldowns.

Kubernetes events lane: forward kube:events involving fluentd, fluent-bit, or fluentbit pods. Filter to high-signal reasons such as BackOff, Unhealthy, Failed, OOMKilled, and Evicted while retaining message text for correlation with node pressure.

Optional sixth lane: when sourcetype mapping lags, run a short Python or bash scripted input that writes the raw /api/plugins.json body to a staging index with checksum deduplication until FIELDALIAS coverage is complete.

props.conf guidance: TIME_FORMAT for ISO-8601 metrics JSON, TRUNCATE at least one million for large plugin dumps, KV_MODE for key-value log lines where appropriate, and SEDCMD to strip ANSI color codes from container logs.

Pre-save validation: index=* sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent earliest=-1h must return queue fields after the first interval; sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics must move when you intentionally pause a non-production HEC token; sourcetype=kube:events must show BackOff during intentional crashloop tests in lab.

Example curl helpers for design reviews:

```bash
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json | head
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics | head
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/fluent-bit --tail=200
kubectl -n logging get events --field-selector involvedObject.kind=Pod | rg -i fluent
```

Dashboards: host a matrix by cluster and shipper_node with lane-colored severity, drilldowns to raw JSON, stderr lines, and kube event messages. Optional Splunk ITSI episodes can bind KPIs to counts of page severity rows per cluster_tier.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster, shipper_node, plugin_id rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action, coalesce_lane, and cond_msg in pager descriptions so incident commanders open shipper runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans the Fluentd monitor lane, Fluentd log lane, Fluent Bit metrics lane, Fluent Bit log lane, and Kubernetes events lane so a silent single sourcetype outage does not hide genuine saturation. coalesce ladders absorb exporter field naming drift across buffer_queue_length, queue_length, q_size, and buffer_queue_byte_size. streamstats measures short-window event arrival burn rate proxies via burn_window counts per merge_key. eventstats adds cluster_fill_p95 hotspot context and cluster_hotspot_cnt escalation hints. case maps combined fill_pct, retry_pct, retry_cnt, and lane-specific cond_msg patterns into page versus warn versus info tiers. recommended_action encodes curl monitor_agent steps, kubectl describe guidance, downstream HEC and Elasticsearch checks, Kafka broker health, S3 endpoint reachability, and storage scheduler reviews without pretending Splunk replaces vendor CLIs.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.1 Fluentd monitor_agent + Fluent Bit metrics multisearch lanes HEC-ES-Kafka correlation; earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,hostname,fluentd_host,""))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,plugin_type,out_plugin,"fluentd_out")))
      | eval buf_q=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_length,queue_length,q_size,buffer_queue_byte_size,"0")),10)
      | eval buf_cap=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_limit,queue_limit,max_queued_chunks,buffer_length_limit,"0")),10)
      | eval fill_pct=if(buf_cap>0,round(100*buf_q/nullif(buf_cap,0),2),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(percent_full,pct_queued,"0")),10))
      | eval retry_cnt=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,retries,plugin_retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_pct=if(retry_cnt<=0,0,round(min(100,retry_cnt/3),2))
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(record_age_sec,oldest_chunk_age_sec,"0")),10)/60
      | eval lane="fluentd_monitor"
      | eval raw_sig=strcat("fdm:",plugin_id,":q=",tostring(round(coalesce(buf_q,0),0)))
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,hostname,""))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"bufferoverflow|buffer.?overflow|bufferoverflowerror|emit transaction failed|failed to flush the buffer|out of buffer")
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"fluentd_log_line")
      | eval fill_pct=92
      | eval retry_cnt=if(match(lr,"retry|exhaust|giving up|too many error"),400,25)
      | eval retry_pct=if(retry_cnt>=100,88,22)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentd_log"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(_raw,1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,name,pod_name,hostname,""))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_name,output,input_name,"fluentbit_metrics")))
      | eval r_ok=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries,output_retries,retry_total,"0")),10)
      | eval r_bad=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries_failed,retries_failed_count,errors,output_dropped_records,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_pct=if(r_ok+r_bad>0,round(100*r_bad/nullif(r_ok+r_bad,0),2),0)
      | eval fill_pct=if(match(lower(_raw),"chunks_overlimit|chunk.*overlimit|overlimit"),97,if(retry_pct>=35,82,18))
      | eval retry_cnt=coalesce(r_bad,r_ok)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_metrics"
      | eval raw_sig=strcat("fbm:",plugin_id,":fail=",tostring(coalesce(r_bad,0)))
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=fluentbit:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,pod_name,""))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"no upstream|upstream connections|failed to flush|output.*error|chunk")
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_name,section,"fluentbit_output_log")
      | eval fill_pct=88
      | eval retry_cnt=if(match(lr,"no upstream"),777,55)
      | eval retry_pct=if(match(lr,"no upstream"),99,41)
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_log"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(_raw,1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=main OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=kube:events earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval inv=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name,involved_object_name,name,""))))
      | where match(inv,"fluentd|fluent-bit|fluentbit")
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason,Reason,Type,type,"")))
      | where match(lower(reason),"backoff|unhealthy|failed|oom|evicted")
      | eval shipper_node=inv
      | eval plugin_id=strcat("kube_evt:",reason)
      | eval fill_pct=72
      | eval retry_cnt=160
      | eval retry_pct=55
      | eval oldest_record_age_min=null()
      | eval lane="kube_events"
      | eval raw_sig=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(message,Message,""))),1,220)
      | fields _time cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane fill_pct retry_pct retry_cnt oldest_record_age_min raw_sig ]
| eval shipper_node=if(len(shipper_node)==0,"unknown_shipper",shipper_node)
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",shipper_node,"|",plugin_id,"|",lane)
| sort 0 + cluster + shipper_node + plugin_id + lane - _time
| streamstats window=9 current=t global=f count AS burn_window BY merge_key
| eval fill_pct=coalesce(fill_pct,0)
| eval retry_cnt=coalesce(retry_cnt,0)
| eval retry_pct=coalesce(retry_pct,0)
| eventstats perc95(fill_pct) AS cluster_fill_p95 BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(fill_pct) AS fill_pct max(retry_pct) AS retry_pct max(retry_cnt) AS retry_cnt max(oldest_record_age_min) AS oldest_record_age_min max(burn_window) AS burn_window max(cluster_fill_p95) AS cluster_fill_p95 first(raw_sig) AS cond_msg first(lane) AS coalesce_lane BY cluster shipper_node plugin_id lane
| eventstats sum(eval(if(fill_pct>=88 OR retry_pct>=70 OR retry_cnt>=500,1,0))) AS cluster_hotspot_cnt BY cluster
| eval severity=case(fill_pct>=98 OR retry_cnt>=800 OR retry_pct>=95,"page", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_log|fluentbit_log") AND (match(cond_msg,"BufferOverflow|no upstream|giving up|failed to flush") OR retry_cnt>=300),"page", fill_pct>=85 OR retry_cnt>=120 OR retry_pct>=50,"warn", cluster_hotspot_cnt>=5,"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_monitor"),"curl_localhost_24220_api_plugins_compare_buffer_fields_then_validate_downstream_splunk_hec", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentd_log"),"trace_emit_flush_errors_disk_io_and_output_tls_hec_es_kafka_s3", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentbit_metrics"),"inspect_fluentbit_storage_chunks_scheduler_and_http_pool_limits", match(coalesce_lane,"fluentbit_log"),"validate_upstream_elasticsearch_kafka_network_policy_and_dns", match(coalesce_lane,"kube_events"),"kubectl_describe_pod_events_check_oom_backoff_probes", true(),"refresh_all_log_shipper_telemetry_lanes")
| join type=left max=0 shipper_node
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval shipper_node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,""))))
     | fields shipper_node cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| table cluster shipper_node plugin_id coalesce_lane fill_pct retry_pct oldest_record_age_min burn_window cluster_hotspot_cnt cluster_fill_p95 severity recommended_action cond_msg cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-logging-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Log shipper buffer saturation $result.severity$ $result.cluster$ $result.shipper_node$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, materialize per-lane summaries hourly into a summary index keyed on cluster, shipper_node, and plugin_id, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large Fluent Bit metric cardinalities, consider indexer-side filtering to outputs with non-zero retries_failed before indexing.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth always starts on-node. curl http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json on a representative Fluentd host and compare buffer_queue_length and retry_count to the Splunk row for the same plugin_id inside the last poll window. When numbers disagree, verify poll interval skew, duplicate host keys, and scripted input authentication failures before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with kubectl -n logging logs for the fluent-bit pod and compare stderr lines mentioning no upstream connections available to sourcetype=fluentbit:log rows. Splunk should mirror transitions within one collection interval; if not, tighten scrape frequency or fix kube-api RBAC for event watchers.

HEC correlation: intentionally pause a non-production HEC token and observe fluentd log lines for emit transaction failed while monitor_agent shows rising retry_count; restore the token and confirm severity returns to info within two windows.

Elasticsearch correlation: induce a non-destructive 429 or disk watermark in lab only and verify fluentd_log lane captures emit failures distinct from Splunk success paths when dual-writing.

Kafka correlation: broker rolling restarts should elevate retries_failed in fluentbit:metrics without immediate chunk loss when storage.max_chunks_up is adequate; confirm Splunk shows warn not page when brokers recover within the burn window.

Synthetic validation: in lab, fill a file buffer slowly under vendor guidance, confirm observability_uc_13_7_1_buffer_retry_saturation returns warn or page with non-null recommended_action, then drain and confirm severity returns to info.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after upgrades settle with quiet queues, low retries, and no kube BackOff events, confirm the alert wrapper returns zero rows for page and warn filters.

Runbook linkage: document expected chunks_overlimit_warning bursts after intentional upstream throttle drills so validators do not file false defects when drills appear in change calendars.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — file-buffer path nearing configured max: compare buffer_total_queued_size to buffer_path filesystem free space, verify inode availability, and expand persistent volume claims or relocate buffer_path to dedicated disks before emit threads stall.

Case 2 — file-buffer disk full on the node: prioritize clearing orphaned chunk files only under vendor-approved procedures, relocate buffers, and relieve concurrent log storms from noisy neighbors sharing the data disk.

Case 3 — memory-buffer overflow during cgroup memory pressure: raise sane container limits, reduce chunk_limit_size, or spill to file storage; correlate with OOMKilled kube events on the same pod.

Case 4 — retry-exhausted output toward Splunk HEC: validate token scope, load balancer health, indexer acknowledgment settings, and TLS interception; rotate tokens under change control when suspicious 403 patterns appear.

Case 5 — retry-exhausted output toward Elasticsearch: check cluster health, disk watermarks, thread pool rejections, and index template mismatches; reduce bulk payload size in the Fluentd output plugin when rejection storms continue.

Case 6 — downstream Kafka broker degraded: inspect under-replicated partitions, controller elections, and SASL authentication failures; tune acks and linger settings only after broker stability returns.

Case 7 — Fluent Bit chunk_overlimit on a single output: increase storage.max_chunks_up cautiously, widen scheduler retry limits with vendor guidance, and verify the troubled output is not stalling on DNS for a single endpoint.

Case 8 — slow persistent volume or noisy neighbor disk contention: measure fsync latency, move buffers to faster storage classes, and separate logging disks from application databases.

Case 9 — container OOMKill for fluent-bit or fluentd: adjust memory limits, reduce parser fan-out, and confirm Kubernetes quality of service classes do not starve logging DaemonSets.

Case 10 — Kubernetes rolling restart causing transient queue spikes: annotate maintenance windows, suppress pages when cluster_hotspot_cnt stays below dual-node blast radius thresholds, and require human acknowledgement only for sustained dwell.

Case 11 — unsupported codec or schema mismatch causing repeated retries: fix formatters at the source, align time_key extraction, and avoid infinite retry loops by capping retry_max_interval under policy.

Case 12 — false calm when monitor_agent endpoint is misconfigured returning empty JSON: alert on absent sourcetype=fluentd:monitor_agent heartbeats per inventory row, compare to expected poll count, and page collection health before trusting all-clear severity.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists fluentd_monitor, fluentd_log, fluentbit_metrics, fluentbit_log, and kube_events arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, and case appear; closing table includes cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment from fluentd_node_inventory.csv alongside operational columns for analysts.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the log forwarders that buffer and resend messages so we notice stuck queues and give-up loops before your logs quietly thin out. We help teams see whether tight memory, full disks, or strained receivers caused it so they fix the right link in the chain.
hardware: Applies to Linux virtual machines and Kubernetes worker nodes running Fluentd gem installations, Fluent Bit DaemonSets, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes collectors, and mixed forwarder fleets that terminate at Splunk HEC, Elasticsearch, Kafka, or S3.
